name: Azlan Ontology Registry
description: Merged ontology library — registry entries co-located with artifacts, organized by series

entries.name: DT Ontology (Decision Tree — Extensibility Pattern Evaluation)
CA Ontology (Competitive Analysis) [DEPRECATED]
entries.deprecationNote: Entities absorbed into ORG-CONTEXT v3.0.0 (Epic 9C). Use org-ctx: namespace equivalents.
entries.deprecationReason: Competitive Analysis entities absorbed into ORG-CONTEXT v3.0.0 CompetitiveLandscape subtree

entries.name: CL Ontology (Competitive Landscape) [DEPRECATED]
entries.deprecationNote: Entities absorbed into ORG-CONTEXT v3.0.0 (Epic 9C). Use org-ctx: namespace equivalents.
entries.deprecationReason: Competitive Landscape entities absorbed into ORG-CONTEXT v3.0.0 CompetitiveLandscape subtree

entries.name: REASON Ontology (MECE, Logic Trees, Hypothesis-Driven Analysis)
MACRO Ontology (PESTEL, Scenarios, Futures Funnel, Backcasting)
PORTFOLIO Ontology (BCG, Three Horizons, Investment Maps, Strategic Lens)
KANO Ontology (Customer Satisfaction Classification — Kano Model)
VIZSTRAT Ontology (Visual Strategy Artefacts & Deck Patterns)
MCSB Ontology (Microsoft Cloud Security Benchmark v1)
GRC-FW Ontology (GRC Framework — Governance, Risk & Compliance)

entries.deprecationReason: Replaced by GRC-FW-ONT v3.0.0 with COBIT 2019/ISO 38500/NIST CSF 2.0 alignment

entries.name: MCSB v2 Ontology (Microsoft Cloud Security Benchmark v2)
Az-ALZ Assessment Ontology (Azure Landing Zone Assessment)
NCSC-CAF Ontology (NCSC Cyber Assessment Framework v4.0)
DSPT Ontology (NHS Data Security and Protection Toolkit)
FAIRSLICE Ontology (FairSlice Platform Economics & Revenue Distribution)

VE-Series.name: Value Engineering Series
VE-Series.description: Strategic value definition, measurement, analysis, and customer role taxonomy ontologies

VSOM-SA.name: VSOM Strategy Analysis
VSOM-SA.description: Strategic analysis frameworks layered on VSOM spine — enables deeper pattern analysis as sub-analysis of VSOM

VSOM-SC.name: VSOM Strategy Communication
VSOM-SC.description: Strategy communication, audience cascade, culture alignment, and visual artefact ontologies — the 'how we tell' complement to VSOM-SA's 'how we think'

EA.name: Enterprise Architecture
EA.description: Hub-spoke EA sub-series. EA-CORE is the platform-agnostic hub. Spokes are methodology-driven (TOGAF), vendor-specific (MSFT, AWS, Google), or capability-driven (AI).

RCSG-Series.name: Governance, Risk & Compliance (GRC) Series
RCSG-Series.description: Governance-centric hub-spoke architecture — GRC-FW as governance hub with risk, compliance, security, AI governance, and resource economics as governed domains. Aligned to COBIT 2019, ISO 38500, NIST CSF 2.0, IIA Three Lines.

GRC-01-GOV.name: Governance Hub
GRC-01-GOV.description: GRC governance hub — GRC-FW framework ontology with governance structures, bodies, policies, assurance, cross-domain edges, 7 VSEM bridges. Governance is superordinate.

GRC-02-RISK.name: Risk Domain
GRC-02-RISK.description: Enterprise and domain-specific risk management ontologies — ERM (ISO 31000, Tier 1) and RMF-IS27005 (ISO 27005, Tier 2)

GRC-03-COMP.name: Compliance Domain
GRC-03-COMP.description: Regulatory compliance and assessment framework ontologies

GRC-04-SEC.name: Security Domain
GRC-04-SEC.description: Security benchmark, data protection, and infrastructure assessment ontologies

ORG-CTX.name: Organisation Context
ORG-CTX.description: Context types, subtypes, and environmental factors

validationSummary.oaaVersionNotes: 42 compliant ontologies at OAA v6.1.0+. KPI at v6.2.0. DT-ONT v1.0.0 added to Orchestration (Decision Tree — 7 hypothesis gates, 12 recommendations, 3 evaluation layers). OFM-ONT v1.0.0 in PE-Series (Order Fulfilment Management). LSC-ONT v1.2.0 in PE-Series (Logistics & Supply Chain). VE-Series expanded with CRT-ONT (Customer Role Taxonomy). EMC-ONT v5.0.0: InstanceConfiguration pattern with GraphScopeRule, ComposedGraphSpec, CanonicalSnapshot. W4M-WWG is second InstanceConfiguration (after BAIV). IND-ONT v1.0.0 (Individual Types; proposal status). 2 deprecated (CA, CL), 2 placeholders (MCSB2, AZALZ), 1 proposal (IND).

pfiInstances.name: BAIV Instance
pfiInstances.description: Brand AI Visibility PF-Instance — SaaS platform for B2B AI citation tracking and content optimisation

instanceDataFiles.description: BAIV AI Visibility Value Proposition instance
BAIV C-Suite role instances with strategic objectives
Enhanced VP+RRR integration with security context
BAIV Enterprise Framework Services security data

pfiInstances.name: RCS Instance
pfiInstances.description: RCS PF-Instance — regulatory compliance and security platform for multi-framework governance

pfiInstances.name: W4M Instance
pfiInstances.description: W4M PF-Instance — SaaS/EA multi-tenant platform with client sub-instances

pfiInstances.name: VHF Nutrition App Instance
pfiInstances.description: VHF Nutrition App PF-Instance — B2C HealthTech product. Simplest valid PFI composition: 6-ontology, 3-pattern, B2C-focused graph. EMC composition pipeline proving ground.

instanceDataFiles.description: VHF Nutrition coaching programmes and meal plans

pfiInstances.name: Antiques & Collectibles Instance
pfiInstances.description: ANTQ PF-Instance — B2C/B2B antiques and insurance valuations platform. Uses IND-ONT (6 B2B + 6 B2C + 2 B2B2C individual type categories, 7 prototype individuals) and ANTIQUES-ONT domain ontology.

pfiInstances.name: AIRL CAF Audit & Advisory
pfiInstances.description: AIRL CAF Audit & Advisory PF-Instance — UK NCSC Cyber Assessment Framework agentic audit, gap analysis, remediation, and cyber insurance readiness platform

pfiInstances.name: W4M WWG (World Wide Gourmet)
pfiInstances.description: W4M WWG PF-Instance — UK specialist food importer with 4 inbound supply corridors (AU, NZ, IS, IE). Second EMC InstanceConfiguration (after BAIV). Supply-demand graph with LSC→Warehouse→OFM bridge.

pfiInstances.name: W4M EOMS
pfiInstances.description: W4M EOMS PF-Instance — SaaS platform product (full ontology suite)

pfiInstances.name: FloodGraph AI — Paul Cowen
pfiInstances.description: PFI-PC-DICE — FloodGraph AI flood risk assessment platform. 4 domain agents (FRA-Assessment, SuDS-Mitigation, Climate-Projection, FRA-Narrative), 4 stakeholder views (Developer, Consultant, LPA, Insurer), GRC-FW compliance, KPI+BSC analytics, PPM project management.
